message.reasoning:
We need to output JSON only with shape: {"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call": {"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}.

We are currently analyzing need to gather exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions. We have expansions done, but need to locate RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION definition. Previously used graph.find@v1 with status ok, path matches one thing. That gave maybe a match. It returned something like status ok, path, line_count:0, match_count:1, fields:[matches,status]. Likely we need to actually retrieve that node's details to see its value. Perhaps we need to expand file or hydrate source? We have already hydrated source earlier. But maybe we need to fetch exact definition. Probably we need to get the source of artifacts.rs to view the constant RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION. We might need to use graph.find again with more specifics, or maybe use graph.path? But we likely need to get the symbol's source code. Could use graph.describe@... but we need to target the symbol identifier. We have symbol identifier RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION defined in artifacts.rs at a certain line maybe near 61? In code window we saw skeleton comment "#61". So maybe we need to fetch that symbol's definition. Use graph.find with name_regex? Or use graph.resolve with selector? The tool contracts: graph.resolve@v1 takes a selector string. If we have the full symbol key maybe we can resolve it to get details. For example selector could be "symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION". That might resolve to the node. Then we could perhaps read its source? There's no direct tool to read code but maybe graph.describe will give description including source? Not sure.

The active code windows show the file content but truncated, didn't show the constant. We might need to expand_file with deeper depth to view that part. We could call session.expand_file with target selector maybe "symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION". That will expand that symbol's source. The frontier recommended expand_file operation on file symbols. So we can do a tool_call session.expand_file@v1 with payload specifying target selector for that symbol.

We need to output JSON with kind tool_call, tool_name, payload. Also acquired_fact_ids list currently empty? We might already have acquired some fact ids earlier? The system hasn't recorded any acquired fact ids yet. We can include maybe empty array. But need to fill acquired_fact_ids with any fact ids we have acquired: maybe "F1"? Not sure. The schema says acquired_fact_ids:["<fact id>"]. We can include something like "S18"? That's the symbol id for RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION. But we haven't acquired it yet; we need to acquire it via tool call. So maybe we leave empty.

Readiness state likely "evidence_visible" if we have some evidence but not ready to edit. Or "not_ready". Probably "evidence_visible". The status prior was "Locating RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION definition". That suggests we have located but not retrieved content. We might be ready to expand file to view content. So readiness_state maybe "evidence_visible"? Or "evidence_acquired"? Since we haven't gotten actual definition yet, maybe still "evidence_visible". But the instruction: "Readiness states: not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". We have evidence visible (we know location) but not actual content. So maybe "evidence_visible". Let's set readiness_state to "evidence_visible".

Now tool_call payload: We need to specify target selector for expansion. Use something like "symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION". Also maybe include depth etc. Minimal payload: {} maybe but we need to include selector. The tool contract: session.expand_file@v1 payload includes target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets. So we can provide "target":"symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION". Keep other fields default.

Thus JSON:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Expanding file to view RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION definition",
  "tool_call": {
    "tool_name":"session.expand_file@v1",
    "payload":{
      "target":"symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION"
    }
  },
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"evidence_visible"
}

Make sure no extra fields. The shape requires "tool_call": {"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{}}; So payload must be object, can include fields. That's fine.

Check that we didn't include any extra keys. The top-level keys: kind, assistant_message, tool_call, acquired_fact_ids, readiness_state. That's correct.

Thus final answer.
